# Feature: session-start

> Scope: scripts/session-start.sh, hooks/hooks.json
> Stack: shell/bash

## What it does

Session startup hook that clears stale runtime state when a new Claude Code session begins. Removes the `.purlin/runtime/invariant_write_lock` file to ensure each session starts with a clean gate — no leftover bypass locks from interrupted invariant syncs.

## Rules

- RULE-1: On session start, `.purlin/runtime/invariant_write_lock` is deleted if it exists
- RULE-2: If the runtime directory does not exist, the hook exits cleanly (exit code 0)
- RULE-3: The hook always exits with code 0 (never blocks session startup)
- RULE-4: The hook is registered in hooks.json as a SessionStart hook matching `startup|clear|compact`

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Create a lock file, run session-start.sh; verify the lock file no longer exists
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Remove the runtime directory, run session-start.sh; verify exit code 0
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Run session-start.sh in various states; verify exit code is always 0
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Parse hooks.json; verify SessionStart entry matches `startup|clear|compact` and runs session-start.sh
